headline: What Digital Realty Trust (DLR)'s Milan and Samsung Colocation Moves Mean For Shareholders
summary: Digital Realty announced it had entered the Milan market with plans for a new data center campus of up to 84 MW, while also supplying Samsung Electronics with enterprise-grade colocation infrastructure at its ICN10 facility in Seoul. These moves expand Digital Realty’s footprint across key European and Asian connectivity hubs, underscoring its role in supporting cloud and AI infrastructure for large technology customers. Next, we’ll examine how the Milan expansion, with its planned 8 MW...
